concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains two handwritten signatures (for Mark F. Richardson and Susan O. Washburn) which are essential visual components of a signed resolution. While the text names are present, the graphical signatures themselves are missing from the HTML output. The official seal is present.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Handwritten signatures appearing above the lines for 'MARK F. RICHARDSON, CHAIRMAN' and 'SUSAN O. WASHBURN'.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML provides the text names but omits the images of the actual signatures.
concerns.location: Signature block at the bottom of the page.

concerns.analysis: The main resolution title is visually duplicated in the HTML output. It appears first inside the <header> tag as an <h1> element and immediately again inside the <main> section as an <h2> element. The source document only displays this title once.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION OF ROCKINGHAM COUNTY 911 EMERGENCY TELECOMMUNICATORS
concerns.output_quote:
RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION OF ROCKINGHAM COUNTY 911 EMERGENCY TELECOMMUNICATORS

and

RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION OF ROCKINGHAM COUNTY 911 EMERGENCY TELECOMMUNICATORS

concerns.analysis: The overall visual quality is slightly degraded due to the omission of the signature lines (horizontal rules) that define the signing block in the original document. This, combined with the missing signatures and duplicated title, reduces the formal polish expected of a government resolution.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Horizontal lines placed above the Chairman's and Clerk's printed names.
concerns.output_quote: The text is present without the supporting horizontal lines used for anchoring signatures.
concerns.location: Signature block at the bottom of the page.
